# NexaLink Synthetic Call Audio — Ground Truth Reference

**Organization:** NexaLink Telecommunications

**Generated By:** ElevenLabs TTS

**Total Files:** 10 audio calls (2 per SOP scenario)

---

## How to Read This File

Each audio file has a full ground truth record below. When running your RAG evaluation system against a call, compare the system's output against the expected values here.

**Fields explained:**
- **Expected Score** — the overall quality score the system should assign (0–5.0 scale)
- **Compliance Score** — percentage of the 10-item checklist the agent completed correctly
- **Sentiment Delta** — how the customer's emotional state should change from start to finish
- **Escalation Decision** — whether an escalation was required and if it happened correctly
- **Key Violations** — specific policy breaches the system must detect to pass the test

## 01a — Refund Request — Good Call
**File:** `01a-refund-request-good.mp3`
**SOP:** 01-refund-request-processing
**Agent:** Sara | **Customer:** David Holloway

### Expected Scores
| Metric | Expected Value |
|---|---|
| Overall Score | 4.7 / 5.0 |
| Compliance Score | 100% |
| Empathy | 5 / 5 |
| Clarity | 4 / 5 |
| Problem Solving | 5 / 5 |
| Product Knowledge | 5 / 5 |
| Policy Adherence | 5 / 5 |

### Sentiment
- **Start:** Frustrated
- **End:** Happy
- **Delta:** Positive ✅

### Escalation Decision
- **Required:** No
- **Correct Action:** Resolved at Tier 1
- **Outcome:** ✅ Correct — no escalation initiated

### Compliance Checklist
| # | Item | Expected |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Proper Greeting | ✅ PASS |
| 2 | Recording Notification | ✅ PASS |
| 3 | Identity Verification | ✅ PASS |
| 4 | Privacy Disclaimer | ✅ PASS |
| 5 | Empathy Statement | ✅ PASS |
| 6 | Validation Protocol | ✅ PASS |
| 7 | Hold Protocol | ✅ PASS |
| 8 | Resolution Summary | ✅ PASS |
| 9 | Additional Assistance | ✅ PASS |
| 10 | Closing & Survey | ✅ PASS |

### Key Detections Required
- Agent correctly identifies promo expiry as root cause
- Applies $25 goodwill credit — within Tier 1 authority limit
- Uses code `ADJ-COURTESY` — correct
- Quotes correct refund timeline (5–10 business days for bill credit)
- No forbidden phrases used

## 01b — Refund Request — Bad Call
**File:** `01b-refund-request-bad.mp3`
**SOP:** 01-refund-request-processing
**Agent:** Mike | **Customer:** Patricia Nguyen

### Expected Scores
| Metric | Expected Value |
|---|---|
| Overall Score | 1.8 / 5.0 |
| Compliance Score | 30% |
| Empathy | 1 / 5 |
| Clarity | 2 / 5 |
| Problem Solving | 2 / 5 |
| Product Knowledge | 2 / 5 |
| Policy Adherence | 1 / 5 |

### Sentiment
- **Start:** Frustrated
- **End:** Angry
- **Delta:** Negative ❌

### Escalation Decision
- **Required:** Yes — customer requested supervisor (mandatory transfer after one de-escalation attempt)
- **Correct Action:** Warm transfer to Tier 3 after first refusal
- **Outcome:** ❌ Incorrect — agent delayed twice, then cold transferred with no briefing

### Compliance Checklist
| # | Item | Expected |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Proper Greeting | ✅ PASS |
| 2 | Recording Notification | ❌ FAIL |
| 3 | Identity Verification | ❌ FAIL |
| 4 | Privacy Disclaimer | ❌ FAIL |
| 5 | Empathy Statement | ❌ FAIL |
| 6 | Validation Protocol | ✅ PASS |
| 7 | Hold Protocol | ❌ FAIL |
| 8 | Resolution Summary | ❌ FAIL |
| 9 | Additional Assistance | ❌ FAIL |
| 10 | Closing & Survey | ✅ PASS |

### Key Violations — System Must Detect All
| Violation | Type | Policy Reference |
|---|---|---|
| Recording notification never stated | CRITICAL | nexalink_data_privacy_policy §3.1 |
| Account accessed with 1 of 5 identifiers only | CRITICAL | nexalink_call_handling_policy §3.1 |
| Forbidden phrase: "That's our policy" | HIGH | nexalink_deescalation_policy §4.2 |
| Forbidden phrase: "There's nothing I can do" | HIGH | nexalink_deescalation_policy §4.2 |
| Forbidden phrase: "Calm down" | HIGH | nexalink_deescalation_policy §4.2 |
| Supervisor request ignored twice | HIGH | nexalink_escalation_routing_policy §2.1 |
| Cold transfer with no briefing | MEDIUM | nexalink_escalation_routing_policy §5.1 |

## 02a — Billing Issue Resolution — Good Call
**File:** `02a-billing-issue-good.mp3`
**SOP:** 02-billing-issue-resolution
**Agent:** Layla | **Customer:** James Okafor

### Expected Scores
| Metric | Expected Value |
|---|---|
| Overall Score | 4.3 / 5.0 |
| Compliance Score | 90% |
| Empathy | 5 / 5 |
| Clarity | 4 / 5 |
| Problem Solving | 5 / 5 |
| Product Knowledge | 4 / 5 |
| Policy Adherence | 4 / 5 |

### Sentiment
- **Start:** Anxious
- **End:** Relieved / Happy
- **Delta:** Positive ✅

### Escalation Decision
- **Required:** No — credit above Tier 1 limit correctly sent to Team Lead via Credit Approval Request
- **Outcome:** ✅ Correct process followed

### Compliance Checklist
| # | Item | Expected |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Proper Greeting | ✅ PASS |
| 2 | Recording Notification | ✅ PASS |
| 3 | Identity Verification | ✅ PASS |
| 4 | Privacy Disclaimer | ✅ PASS |
| 5 | Empathy Statement | ✅ PASS |
| 6 | Validation Protocol | ✅ PASS |
| 7 | Hold Protocol | ✅ PASS |
| 8 | Resolution Summary | ✅ PASS |
| 9 | Additional Assistance | ✅ PASS |
| 10 | Closing & Survey | ❌ FAIL — no case reference number provided |

### Key Detections Required
- $95 charge correctly identified as unauthorised technician visit (billing error)
- Credit Approval Request submitted to Tier 3 — correct for amount above $25
- Proactively diagnosed secondary issue (slow internet) during same call
- Minor gap: case reference number missing — system should flag this

## 02b — Billing Issue Resolution — Bad Call
**File:** `02b-billing-issue-bad.mp3`
**SOP:** 02-billing-issue-resolution
**Agent:** Tom | **Customer:** Sandra Mills

### Expected Scores
| Metric | Expected Value |
|---|---|
| Overall Score | 2.4 / 5.0 |
| Compliance Score | 60% |
| Empathy | 2 / 5 |
| Clarity | 2 / 5 |
| Problem Solving | 2 / 5 |
| Product Knowledge | 1 / 5 |
| Policy Adherence | 2 / 5 |

### Sentiment
- **Start:** Frustrated
- **End:** Angry / Unresolved
- **Delta:** Negative ❌

### Escalation Decision
- **Required:** Yes — $220 dispute exceeds Tier 1 authority ($25), must go to Tier 2
- **Correct Action:** Escalate to Tier 2 immediately after confirming dispute amount
- **Outcome:** ❌ Agent attempted to apply $220 credit at Tier 1 — system blocked it, agent had no protocol awareness

### Compliance Checklist
| # | Item | Expected |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Proper Greeting | ✅ PASS |
| 2 | Recording Notification | ✅ PASS |
| 3 | Identity Verification | ✅ PASS |
| 4 | Privacy Disclaimer | ❌ FAIL |
| 5 | Empathy Statement | ❌ FAIL |
| 6 | Validation Protocol | ✅ PASS |
| 7 | Hold Protocol | ❌ FAIL — 18 seconds dead air, no verbal filler |
| 8 | Resolution Summary | ❌ FAIL |
| 9 | Additional Assistance | ✅ PASS |
| 10 | Closing & Survey | ✅ PASS |

### Key Violations — System Must Detect All
| Violation | Type | Policy Reference |
|---|---|---|
| Attempted $220 credit at Tier 1 (limit is $25) | CRITICAL | nexalink_refund_and_compensation_policy §4.0 |
| Forbidden phrase: "I don't know" | HIGH | nexalink_deescalation_policy §4.2 |
| 18 seconds dead air without filler | MEDIUM | nexalink_call_handling_policy §5.4 |
| Incorrect refund timeline quoted (3–5 days, correct is 15–30 days) | HIGH | nexalink_refund_and_compensation_policy §7.0 |
| Privacy disclaimer missing | HIGH | nexalink_data_privacy_policy §3.2 |
| Case closed as resolved when issue was unresolved | HIGH | nexalink_call_handling_policy §6.2 |

## 03a — Technical Support — Good Call
**File:** `03a-technical-support-good.mp3`
**SOP:** 03-technical-support-troubleshooting
**Agent:** Rania | **Customer:** Kevin Marsh

### Expected Scores
| Metric | Expected Value |
|---|---|
| Overall Score | 4.8 / 5.0 |
| Compliance Score | 100% |
| Empathy | 5 / 5 |
| Clarity | 5 / 5 |
| Problem Solving | 5 / 5 |
| Product Knowledge | 5 / 5 |
| Policy Adherence | 5 / 5 |

### Sentiment
- **Start:** Angry (work impact)
- **End:** Happy
- **Delta:** Strong Positive ✅

### Escalation Decision
- **Required:** No — resolved at Tier 1 through correct Path B diagnosis
- **Outcome:** ✅ Correct

### Compliance Checklist
All 10 items: ✅ PASS

### Key Detections Required
- Outage map checked before device-level troubleshooting
- Time-of-day pattern identified as congestion indicator (good probing)
- Speed test result: 42/100 Mbps — below threshold but not yet at 50% floor for escalation
- Resolution confirmed via live speed test during call (89 Mbps after fix)
- Root cause explained clearly: congested 2.4GHz channel + router memory
- No forbidden phrases, no interruptions

---

## 03b — Technical Support — Bad Call
**File:** `03b-technical-support-bad.mp3`
**SOP:** 03-technical-support-troubleshooting
**Agent:** Chris | **Customer:** Mei Chen

### Expected Scores
| Metric | Expected Value |
|---|---|
| Overall Score | 2.6 / 5.0 |
| Compliance Score | 60% |
| Empathy | 1 / 5 |
| Clarity | 2 / 5 |
| Problem Solving | 2 / 5 |
| Product Knowledge | 2 / 5 |
| Policy Adherence | 2 / 5 |

### Sentiment
- **Start:** Anxious
- **End:** Frustrated / Unresolved
- **Delta:** Negative ❌

### Escalation Decision
- **Required:** Yes — speed at 22/50 Mbps = 44% of plan (below 50% threshold) after reboot → mandatory Tier 2 line test
- **Correct Action:** Escalate to Tier 2 for line test after second speed test confirms below-threshold result
- **Outcome:** ❌ Agent told customer to "wait and see" — escalation trigger missed

### Compliance Checklist
| # | Item | Expected |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Proper Greeting | ✅ PASS |
| 2 | Recording Notification | ❌ FAIL — stated after verification, not at call start |
| 3 | Identity Verification | ✅ PASS |
| 4 | Privacy Disclaimer | ❌ FAIL |
| 5 | Empathy Statement | ❌ FAIL |
| 6 | Validation Protocol | ✅ PASS |
| 7 | Hold Protocol | ✅ PASS |
| 8 | Resolution Summary | ✅ PASS — partial |
| 9 | Additional Assistance | ✅ PASS |
| 10 | Closing & Survey | ❌ FAIL |

### Key Violations — System Must Detect All
| Violation | Type | Policy Reference |
|---|---|---|
| Recording notification out of order (after account access) | CRITICAL | nexalink_data_privacy_policy §3.1 |
| Speed 44% of plan after reboot — Tier 2 escalation trigger ignored | CRITICAL | nexalink_product_and_troubleshooting_guide §5.2 |
| Forbidden phrase equivalent: "might just fix itself" | HIGH | nexalink_deescalation_policy §4.2 |
| Customer interrupted 4 times (max allowed: 2) | MEDIUM | nexalink_call_handling_policy §5.3 |
| Case closed as resolved when customer stated issue unresolved | HIGH | nexalink_call_handling_policy §6.2 |
| No empathy despite customer describing vulnerable household situation | MEDIUM | nexalink_call_handling_policy §4.1 |

## 04a — Account Access Recovery — Good Call
**File:** `04a-account-access-good.mp3`
**SOP:** 04-account-access-recovery
**Agent:** Fatima | **Customer:** Robert Ellis

### Expected Scores
| Metric | Expected Value |
|---|---|
| Overall Score | 4.6 / 5.0 |
| Compliance Score | 100% |
| Empathy | 5 / 5 |
| Clarity | 5 / 5 |
| Problem Solving | 5 / 5 |
| Product Knowledge | 4 / 5 |
| Policy Adherence | 5 / 5 |

### Sentiment
- **Start:** Anxious
- **End:** Happy / Relieved
- **Delta:** Strong Positive ✅

### Escalation Decision
- **Required:** No — alternative verification handled correctly at Tier 1
- **Outcome:** ✅ Correct

### Compliance Checklist
All 10 items: ✅ PASS

### Key Detections Required
- Standard 3-of-5 verification failed (customer could not provide PIN)
- Correctly used alternative path: email OTP + payment card confirmation
- PIN reset performed — new PIN entered via keypad, NOT spoken aloud
- PIN not recorded in case notes — full compliance
- Online portal password handled via self-service link — correct protocol
- Payment details verified as secondary issue during same call
- No security shortcuts taken despite customer inability to verify normally

## 04b — Account Access Recovery — Bad Call
**File:** `04b-account-access-bad.mp3`
**SOP:** 04-account-access-recovery
**Agent:** Dan | **Customer:** Unknown / Unverified Caller

### Expected Scores
| Metric | Expected Value |
|---|---|
| Overall Score | 1.5 / 5.0 |
| Compliance Score | 20% |
| Empathy | 1 / 5 |
| Clarity | 2 / 5 |
| Problem Solving | 1 / 5 |
| Product Knowledge | 1 / 5 |
| Policy Adherence | 1 / 5 |

### Sentiment
- **Start:** Neutral
- **End:** Neutral (caller achieved fraudulent goal — no frustration)
- **Delta:** Flat — caller is satisfied, legitimate customer may be compromised ❌

### Escalation Decision
- **Required:** Yes — zero verification completed, all access changes must be refused and customer directed to store
- **Correct Action:** Refuse all account changes, do not confirm account existence to unverified caller
- **Outcome:** ❌ Complete failure — PIN reset, email, account number, and card details all disclosed to unverified caller

### Compliance Checklist
| # | Item | Expected |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Proper Greeting | ✅ PASS |
| 2 | Recording Notification | ❌ FAIL |
| 3 | Identity Verification | ❌ FAIL — ZERO identifiers verified |
| 4 | Privacy Disclaimer | ❌ FAIL |
| 5 | Empathy Statement | ❌ FAIL |
| 6 | Validation Protocol | ❌ FAIL |
| 7 | Hold Protocol | ✅ PASS |
| 8 | Resolution Summary | ❌ FAIL |
| 9 | Additional Assistance | ❌ FAIL |
| 10 | Closing & Survey | ✅ PASS |

### Key Violations — System Must Detect All
| Violation | Type | Policy Reference |
|---|---|---|
| Recording notification never stated | CRITICAL | nexalink_data_privacy_policy §3.1 |
| Account existence confirmed to unverified caller | CRITICAL | nexalink_call_handling_policy §3.1 |
| PIN reset with ZERO verified identifiers | CRITICAL | nexalink_call_handling_policy §3.1 |
| New PIN read back verbally over the phone | CRITICAL | nexalink_data_privacy_policy §4.2 |
| Email address disclosed to unverified caller | CRITICAL | nexalink_data_privacy_policy §4.1 |
| Full account number disclosed to unverified caller | CRITICAL | nexalink_data_privacy_policy §4.1 |
| Payment card last 4 digits disclosed unprompted | CRITICAL | nexalink_data_privacy_policy §4.1 |
| ⚠️ NOTE: This call is a social engineering simulation — all 5 disclosures must be flagged | CRITICAL | — |

## 05a — Retention & Cancellation — Good Call
**File:** `05a-retention-good.mp3`
**SOP:** 05-other-common-case
**Agent:** Nadia | **Customer:** Brian Cole

### Expected Scores
| Metric | Expected Value |
|---|---|
| Overall Score | 4.7 / 5.0 |
| Compliance Score | 100% |
| Empathy | 5 / 5 |
| Clarity | 5 / 5 |
| Problem Solving | 5 / 5 |
| Product Knowledge | 4 / 5 |
| Policy Adherence | 5 / 5 |

### Sentiment
- **Start:** Angry
- **End:** Hopeful
- **Delta:** Strong Positive ✅

### Escalation Decision
- **Required:** Yes — cancellation must be processed by Tier 3 Retention
- **Correct Action:** Single retention attempt, then warm transfer to Tier 3 with full briefing
- **Outcome:** ✅ Correct — warm transfer with complete briefing performed

### Compliance Checklist
All 10 items: ✅ PASS

### Key Detections Required
- Single retention attempt made — no repetition or multiple offers
- Technical issue (slow internet) identified and resolved during retention call — correct behaviour
- Cancellation not processed at Tier 1 — correctly handed to Tier 3
- Warm transfer includes: root cause, offer made, outcome, sentiment, account tenure
- Survey correctly withheld (call transferred — not appropriate context for survey)
- Case reference provided before transfer

---

## 05b — Retention & Cancellation — Bad Call
**File:** `05b-retention-bad.mp3`
**SOP:** 05-other-common-case
**Agent:** Gary | **Customer:** Helen Voss

### Expected Scores
| Metric | Expected Value |
|---|---|
| Overall Score | 2.1 / 5.0 |
| Compliance Score | 40% |
| Empathy | 2 / 5 |
| Clarity | 1 / 5 |
| Problem Solving | 1 / 5 |
| Product Knowledge | 1 / 5 |
| Policy Adherence | 1 / 5 |

### Sentiment
- **Start:** Frustrated
- **End:** Angry
- **Delta:** Negative ❌

### Escalation Decision
- **Required:** Yes — cancellation must go to Tier 3; supervisor request is also a mandatory escalation trigger
- **Correct Action:** One retention attempt → warm transfer to Tier 3
- **Outcome:** ❌ Made 4 offers, ignored supervisor request, then processed cancellation at Tier 1

### Compliance Checklist
| # | Item | Expected |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Proper Greeting | ✅ PASS |
| 2 | Recording Notification | ✅ PASS |
| 3 | Identity Verification | ✅ PASS |
| 4 | Privacy Disclaimer | ❌ FAIL |
| 5 | Empathy Statement | ❌ FAIL |
| 6 | Validation Protocol | ❌ FAIL |
| 7 | Hold Protocol | ❌ FAIL |
| 8 | Resolution Summary | ❌ FAIL |
| 9 | Additional Assistance | ❌ FAIL |
| 10 | Closing & Survey | ✅ PASS |

### Key Violations — System Must Detect All
| Violation | Type | Policy Reference |
|---|---|---|
| Forbidden phrase: "You can't cancel without paying a fee" as opener | CRITICAL | nexalink_deescalation_policy §4.2 |
| ETF quoted as flat $200 — incorrect (correct: $20 × months remaining) | HIGH | nexalink_product_and_troubleshooting_guide §4.2 |
| Four retention offers made — policy allows one | HIGH | nexalink_escalation_routing_policy §3.0 |
| Supervisor request ignored | HIGH | nexalink_escalation_routing_policy §2.1 |
| Cancellation processed at Tier 1 — strictly prohibited | CRITICAL | nexalink_escalation_routing_policy §3.0 |
| Competitor pricing confirmed ("I know they charge less") | MEDIUM | nexalink_deescalation_policy §5.0 |
| Hold taken without customer permission | MEDIUM | nexalink_call_handling_policy §5.4 |
| Agent contradicted himself about ETF in same call | HIGH | — |
